source: Learning bash with Rocky - Tests: Return codes, exit, and testing files

questions.q: By convention, a command's return code of 0 means...
questions.why: 0 = ran correctly; a non-zero code signals a problem.

questions.q: Which special variable holds the return code of the last command?
questions.why: $? stores the exit/status code, updated after each command.

questions.q: Which return code does 'command not found' produce (per the examples)?
How do you set a script's own return code?
options: exit N (the exit command with a numeric argument, 0-255)
questions.why: exit [n] ends the script with return code n.

questions.q: For a test, what do the return codes 0, 1 and 2 mean?
options: 0 = true, 1 = false, 2 = the test was not executed correctly
questions.why: $?=0 true, $?=1 false, $?=2 test not correctly executed.

questions.q: Which test option checks that a file exists and is a directory?
questions.why: -d = directory; -e = exists; -f = exists and is a regular file.

questions.q: Which is the modern, more capable internal test syntax in bash?
questions.why: [[ ]] is the bash built-in test, with more features than the external test / [ ].

questions.q: Which operator tests whether file1 is newer than file2?
